summary.intent: Address Codex code review findings on PR #694 of fogodev/ars-ui, push fixes, then monitor for and resolve any subsequent review findings from a re-triggered Codex pass.
summary.outcome: Fixed four issues from the first Codex pass (RatingGroup aria-labelledby, SyncProps tombstone reconciliation, disabled hidden inputs, unchanged rating no-op transitions), then addressed a second-pass finding by adding RTL keyboard navigation to TagGroup's on_tag_keydown helper, with full coverage remaining above the 90% branch threshold.

repo: Coverage gate is `cargo xtask ci coverage`, which runs a merged native + wasm instrumented pipeline across multiple crates including ars-components, ars-dom, ars-i18n, ars-leptos, and ars-dioxus.
Branch coverage threshold for ars-components is 90%; post-RTL-fix the crate was at 90.1% branch and 98.5% line.
Codex reviews are triggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment; active review is indicated by an 'eyes' reaction from the codex user; completion drops the reaction.
Review thread resolution uses the GitHub GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation with PRRT_ prefixed thread IDs.
The repo carries Arabic locale fixtures sufficient to drive RTL direction in unit tests (used to write the TagGroup RTL regression).
TagGroup's on_tag_keydown helper had locale in context but treated horizontal arrow keys as LTR-only, violating the RTL keyboard spec.

code.finding: on_tag_keydown keyboard helper did not consult locale direction; RTL fix swaps left/right arrow semantics when resolved locale direction is RTL.
Unit test block for TagGroup; RTL regression test was added here using an Arabic locale environment.

workflow: When llvm-profdata reports a corrupted wasm .profraw during coverage merge, the correct fix is to remove only generated coverage artifacts (`target/wasm-coverage` and `target/coverage`) and rerun—not to skip coverage.
Polling Codex review status via reactions API (`/issues/{n}/reactions`) and unresolved thread GraphQL query is more reliable than waiting on PR review state alone.
Format check (`cargo +nightly fmt --all --check`) should be run before committing; the RTL change triggered an import wrap that needed formatting.

friction: llvm-profdata reported a corrupted ars-dioxus wasm .profraw file during the first coverage merge attempt, requiring artifact cleanup and a full rerun (~10+ minute delay).
Codex review cycle requires extended polling (2-minute intervals) with no push notification on completion, leading to many sleep/poll iterations before the result was available.
The Codex eyes reaction dropping without a thumbs-up reaction required an additional thread + comment inspection step to determine whether the pass was approved or produced new findings.
open_items: Wasm coverage .profraw corruption is a known recurring artifact issue; no permanent fix was applied—cleanup-and-retry is the current workaround.

The four fixes from commit b83e36d0 (RatingGroup label, SyncProps tombstones, disabled inputs, unchanged rating no-op) were pushed but a subsequent full Codex approval was not yet confirmed at the end of this transcript.
